checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated text. All headings and list items present in the JSON correspond to entries in the source PDF agenda.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (350 773 333#) in the output match the source document exactly.
Both pages are represented in the output. While several sub-sections are truncated (missing content), the high-level sections and pages themselves are present.

The source document uses a tabbed structure that resembles a table. The pipeline captures the first 6 items in a table format and the subsequent items as lists. This is an acceptable reformatting choice, though content is missing within those lists (evaluated in C5). The data rows present in the JSON table match the first few rows of the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis:
There are major omissions of content within several sections. 
1. Section 4 (Consent Agenda) is missing all financial data (balances and disbursements) for the NG 911 Reserve Fund, CMRS Account, PSAP Account, and PSAP Grant Account. 
2. Section 5 (Executive Director's Report) is missing all its sub-items (a-f).
3. Section 9 (Grant Committee Report) is missing all sub-items (a-c) including specific county project updates.
4. Section 13 (Education Committee Report) is missing all sub-items (a-d).
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 4b: June 2022 Fund balance $ 76,125,411 [through] July 2022 Disbursement $ 2,419,955. Item 5: a) Biennial Audit Status [through] f) Eastern Region PSAP Visits. Item 9: a) PSAP Grant Program Progress Reports... b) FY2023 PSAP Grant Recommendations... c) General Committee Report.
checks.b_evidence_output: The content under 'b) NG 911 Reserve Fund –' is empty. Section 5 'Executive Director’s Report' has no sub-items. Section 9 'Grant Committee Report' has no sub-items.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the significant omissions noted in C5, the text that was successfully converted accurately reflects the wording of the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The content of the document is presented linearly and corresponds to the original source without duplication of text blocks or sections across pages.
The output uses an H1 for the 'AGENDA' title and an H2 for the 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING' subtitle. This hierarchy is logical and correctly follows the document's structure.

The agenda items at the start of the document are rendered in a table format that identifies three headers ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter'). However, the data rows fail to follow this structure. In some rows (e.g., Call to Order), all content is merged into a single cell, whereas the headers imply three columns. In other rows (e.g., Item 1), the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' content are merged into the second cell, leaving the third column empty or non-existent in the data mapping. This is a structural corruption as columns are merged incorrectly compared to the source layout.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source identifies columns for Tab, Topic (Chair's Opening Remarks), and Presenter (Jim Weaver).
checks.b_evidence_output: The data row for item 1 in element index 4 contains only two cells: cell 1 with '1.' and cell 2 with 'Chair's Opening Remarks...Jim Weaver'.

checks.a_analysis: The document flow follows the logical progression of the agenda from headers to the list of items. While some sub-content is missing, the content that is present is in the correct order.

The conversion of lists is incomplete and incorrect. While sub-items for item 4 are captured, the nested sub-items for items 5, 9, and 13 are completely omitted from the output. The source document clearly shows itemized sub-points (e.g., 5a-f, bulleted items under 9a, and 13a-d), but the output renders these as single flat list items for the parent topic only, losing the list structure and its associated content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 includes sub-items a) Biennial Audit Status through f) Eastern Region PSAP Visits. Item 9 has sub-bullets and sub-items a-c. Item 13 has sub-items a-d.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list items for item 5 (reading_order 6) and items 9 and 13 (reading_order 9) contain only the top-level parent text with no nested list structure or sub-item content.

checks.a_analysis: Phone numbers and conference IDs are rendered as plain text, and the agenda packet link mentioned in the text is not hyperlinked. While this is a loss of interactivity, no incorrect or fabricated links were injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML rendering significantly disrupts the visual flow and organization of the document. The agenda, which is a continuous list in the source PDF, is split into a table for the first few items and a bulleted list for others. Furthermore, a large image is inserted in the middle of item 5, separating it from its sub-item (g). Most critically, sub-items 5(a) through 5(f) are visually absent from the HTML rendering, representing a major failure to represent the source layout accurately.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF (pages 1-2) shows a continuous agenda list. Item 5 'Executive Director's Report' is immediately followed by sub-items (a) through (f) on page 1, and (g) on page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows Item 5 at the end of a list, followed by a large logo, then a new list starting with item (g). Sub-items (a) through (f) are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 5, between pages 1 and 2 of the PDF equivalent area in the HTML.

checks.a_analysis: There are two major issues with tabular data rendering. First, the 'Tab Topic Presenter' table at the start of the agenda is misaligned; the 'Presenter' column is empty, and the presenter names (e.g., 'Jim Weaver') have been incorrectly merged into the 'Topic' column. Second, the financial data in sections 4(b) through 4(e), which has a clear tabular structure in the PDF, is completely missing from the HTML output, which only displays the top-level labels.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1 shows a 3-column header for the agenda. Section 4(b) contains indented financial data: 'June 2022 Fund balance $ 76,125,411', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML table shows 'Presenter' column is blank. Item 4(b) in the list shows only '- b) NG 911 Reserve Fund –' with no following data or dollar amounts.
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda table and Item 4 sub-list on page 1.

checks.a_analysis: The NC DIT 911 Board logo is present in the document. While it is resized and repositioned, it is not missing or broken.

No significant visual duplication of body text or meaningful content blocks was observed. The logo appears twice, but this is appropriate given its presence in both the header and footer of the source document.
